figure.title: RX Path Security Architecture
figure.purpose: Shows the cryptographic key and counter management for the receive (RX) path feeding into the AES-GCM Crypto Engine.

technical_summary: Similar to the TX path, but manages contexts per source accelerator.
A Key Derivation Function (KDF) uses Active & alternate master keys for source accelerators (32Bx1023x2).
For each source accelerator, the engine receives derived keys (32Bx1023x2), IV counters (4Bx1023), and epoch counters (8Bx1023) for Request, Read Response, and Write Response streams.
These inputs feed into the AES-GCM Crypto Engine to decrypt and authenticate the RX data.

components.name: AES-GCM Crypto Engine
components.description: Hardware decryption/authentication block for the RX path.

critical_facts: The RX security architecture maintains separate cryptographic contexts for each source accelerator to ensure secure and isolated data reception.
